markdown:
#  What is a Drawing Set? 
##  Answer 
When you upload drawings to Procore, you choose a new or existing _drawing set_ to associate with the collection of drawings. Drawings in the same set will generally have the same drawing date or serve the same purpose, such as being revisions to previous drawing versions. Examples of common drawing sets are Bidding, Issued for Construction, or Amendment/Addendum.
While the Drawings tool shows 'Current Drawings' by default, you can view a specific set of drawings by clicking the 'Drawing Sets' view. See [View Drawings](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/view-drawings). While a drawing _set_ is used to label and organize drawings, a drawing _area_ is an optional feature that allows you to completely separate drawings within a project. See [What are Drawing Areas?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-drawing-areas)
## See Also

  * [Can I upload drawings with the same revision?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-can-i-upload-drawings-with-the-same-revision)
title: What is a Drawing Set?
description: An explanation of a Drawing Set in Procore.
